title: Windows Editions, Requirements, Lifecycle, and Migration Decisions
description: A mixed Windows 10 and Windows 11 fleet includes unsupported hardware, specialized applications, domain needs, encryption requirements, accessibility settings, and pressure to bypass eligibility checks. Learners cumulatively produce Windows support and migration decision packet through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Compare current Windows edition capabilities for identity, management, encryption, virtualization, remote access, updates, hardware, and licensing.
Evaluate processor, architecture, memory, storage, firmware, UEFI, Secure Boot capability, TPM, graphics, display, internet, and account requirements.
Treat Windows 10 exam knowledge as a dated legacy-support and migration case after its general support end, with current exception lookup.
